description: Target rule definitions for Soroban (Rust/Stellar) contract analysis aligned with docs/graph_schema_logic_rust.md. Not wired to adapters until rust_soroban profile ships.

rules.title: Missing Authorization on Public Entry That Mutates State
rules.rationale: Soroban relies on explicit auth. Public impl methods that write ledger-backed storage without Address::require_auth or require_auth_for_args allow any caller to change privileged parameters or steal funds.
preconditions: Callable is reachable as an external/public contract interface under #[contractimpl].
At least one path performs env.storage persistent/instance bump-set style mutation (adapter-defined exact API surface).
rules.detection_logic: Flag pub fn under #[contractimpl] that mutate via env.storage() instance or persistent accessors without Address::require_auth(), require_auth(), or require_auth_for_args(...) on control paths reaching those mutations.
false_positive_patterns: Intentionally permissionless endpoints (simple swap helpers, onboarding flows).
Initialize paths guarded by uninitialized flag or immutable admin constraint.
Read-only wrappers that load storage without writes.
rules.protocol_limits_note: Verify auth surface against current Soroban SDK semantics.
rules.remediation_hint: Require authorization on every externally reachable path that modifies critical storage; split read-only helpers from mutating endpoints.
rules.graph_hint: function node lacks incoming auth_check edge or lacks authorization-related metadata paired with outbound storage-write edges.
rules.minimal_example_bad: pub fn set_admin(env: Env, a: Address) { env.storage().instance().set(&KEY, &a); }
rules.minimal_example_good: pub fn set_admin(env: Env, admin: Address) { admin.require_auth(); env.storage().instance().set(&KEY, &admin); }

rules.title: Potentially Unbounded Structures in Instance Storage
rules.rationale: Instance entries are loaded eagerly for interactions. Uncontrolled growth balloons gas for all callers and risks hitting ledger entry caps (past guidance near 64KiB depending on protocol version).
preconditions: Value type carries variable-sized container such as Vec, Map, Bytes, String, or nested collections.
rules.detection_logic: Match env.storage().instance().set (or bump equivalent) whose payload type resolves to dynamically sized Soroban collection without explicit entry-size bound or chunked strategy.
false_positive_patterns: Fixed-capacity structs or capped tiny Vec constants.
Migration paths that truncate or migrate data into bounded records.
rules.protocol_limits_note: Concrete byte and instruction ceilings depend on deployed network/protocol; treat limits as externally configured facts when scanning.
rules.remediation_hint: Persist large aggregates in Persistent storage with chunked keys or stream processing; enforce max length on instance payloads.
rules.graph_hint: function_to_storage edge to instance-backed state typed as compound collection without bound metadata.
rules.minimal_example_good: // Store bounded counters in instance and shard map keys under persistent()

rules.title: Fallible External Call Without Controlled Error Boundary
rules.rationale: Non-try invokes trap the whole transaction-style failure modes in host; callers cannot branch to partial unwind or compensated refund logic often needed in DeFi composing.
preconditions: Contract invokes another via env.invoke_contract or equivalent infallible host API used by toolchain.
rules.detection_logic: Presence of env.invoke_contract without paired try_invoke style API or downstream Result handling modeled by analyzer when available.
false_positive_patterns: Design where any external revert must revert entire ledger change set.
Wrapper already lowering to Result in macro-generated shim (verify SDK version).
rules.remediation_hint: Prefer try_invoke_contract and propagate errors; reserve trapping invoke only when unconditional abort semantics are deliberate.
rules.graph_hint: cross-contract invoke edge with no succeeding error-handling branch modeled in CFG.

rules.title: Complex Collection Inputs Without Validation
rules.rationale: Deep or malformed guest-provided composites can abort conversion paths, burning fees and weakening availability; low-level Val ingestion without disciplined typing is brittle.
preconditions: pub callable accepts Vec-like or Map-like parameters not obviously wrapped by #[contracttype]-generated safe conversions.
No explicit bounded checks on length/shape preceding use.
rules.detection_logic: Flag pub exported methods taking Vec<Map<...>>, nested collections, or raw Val-ish parameters without precondition checks or limited custom type aliases.
false_positive_patterns: Arguments are #[contracttype] generated types enforcing host-side conversion helpers.
Immediate guard via len() <= const or iterators over bounded enums.
rules.remediation_hint: Model inputs as contract types, validate lengths/nesting, or split batched payloads with explicit caps.
rules.graph_hint: function node binds collection parameters lacking checks edge to validating helper.
rules.minimal_example_bad: pub fn batch(env: Env, items: Vec<Item>) { for i in items { ... } }

rules.title: Persistent or Instance Writes Without Nearby TTL Strategy
rules.rationale: Archival TTL policies can relocate cold state; forgetting renewals freezes user-visible balances until off-chain/SDK restoration.
preconditions: Stateful write uses env.storage persistent or instance APIs subject to TTL/archival semantics for target ledger.
rules.detection_logic: Heuristic when storage sets occur without reachable extend_ttl across intra-procedural CFG or flagged cross-function TTL renewer (confidence medium/low).
false_positive_patterns: Temporary-only storage deletes instead of archiving.
Dedicated keeper/cron documented out-of-band renewing TTL safely.
rules.remediation_hint: Pair writes with deterministic renew helpers or periodic extension flows; document off-chain TTL keepers explicitly.
rules.graph_hint: Persistent/Instance state nodes without modeled extend_ttl linkage in same compilation unit heuristic.

rules.title: Bare panic! Instead of Structured Contract Errors
rules.rationale: panic_with_error! surfaces typed contract errors aiding fuzzers (for example tooling like OrCa) to separate expected logic aborts from unexpected faults; naked panic!/assert hides that signal.
preconditions: Macro appears in shipped contract crate (exclude #[cfg(test)] trees).
rules.detection_logic: Match panic!, assert!, debug_assert! in contract logic excluding test-only gated modules.
false_positive_patterns: Code under #[cfg(test)] or fuzz-only harness crates.
Unreachable internal invariants guarded by exhaustive matches when provably unreachable.
rules.audit_weight_note: Informational/low for release safety; elevate for fuzzing pipelines.
rules.remediation_hint: Replace with panic_with_error! and canonical ContractError enums where applicable.
rules.graph_hint: function node flagged has_panic without structured panic_with_error!

rules.title: Loop Performing Repeated Ledger Reads
rules.rationale: Storage reads dominate resource accounting; iterating keys or querying inside tight loops burns IO budget and risks instruction overflows for realistic ledger caps.
preconditions: Loop executes env.storage getter or enumerator each iteration.
Iteration upper bound depends on ledger-sized collections without static small constant proof.
rules.detection_logic: CFG contains back-edge guarded loop body that always performs storage reads without constant max iterations proven by analyzer.
false_positive_patterns: Compiler-time constant loops with tiny fixed bounds.
Preloaded snapshot fetched once outside loop reused inside.
rules.protocol_limits_note: Historical protocol guidance cited 100 ledger reads per tx and tight IO budgets; confirm current limits for deployments.
rules.remediation_hint: Batch-fetch or paginate; store indices off-chain or cap fan-out with explicit deterministic limits.
rules.graph_hint: function_to_storage read edge occurring inside cyclic control-flow region.
rules.minimal_example_good: Load bounded page once; iterate in-memory slice with MAX_PAGE.

rules.title: Constructor-Like Entry Missing Reinit Guards
rules.rationale: Mis-modeled initializer around deploy and wasm upgrade migrations can reopen admin takeover if admin slot can be rewritten without precondition checks.
preconditions: Symbol matches __constructor or project-specific initializer flagged by conventions.
Function writes privileged role key without asserting prior unset state.
rules.detection_logic: Flag initializer-shaped entry writing admin-critical storage without assert-not-initialized predicate or keyed migration token.
false_positive_patterns: Upgrade-only migration guarded by multisig-managed flag stored immutably after first deploy.
rules.protocol_limits_note: Exact constructor/migration semantics depend on Soroban SDK/version; reconcile rule with release notes before enforcement.
rules.remediation_hint: Enforce exactly-once init and separate migration entrypoints with audited auth and versioned state transitions.
rules.graph_hint: function resembling __constructor writing admin state lacks already_initialized check edge.
rules.minimal_example_bad: pub fn __constructor(env: Env, admin: Address) { env.storage().instance().set(&ADMIN, &admin); }
rules.minimal_example_good: Ensure admin slot empty or guarded by migrate-only privilege before write.
